Workshop.title: Workshop
Workshop.description: Represents a mechanic workshop registered in the application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the workshop entity.

description.description: Detailed description of the workshop and its services.

latitude.description: Latitude coordinate of the workshop's location.

longitude.description: Longitude coordinate of the workshop's location.

obdScannerService.description: Indicates whether the workshop offers OBDII scanner services.

ownerId.description: The UID of the user who owns this workshop.

averageRating.description: The average rating of the workshop, from 1 to 5.

reviewCount.description: The total number of reviews for the workshop.

Appointment.title: Appointment
Appointment.description: Represents a customer appointment at a workshop.

id.description: Unique identifier for the appointment entity.

workshopId.description: Reference to the Workshop. (Relationship: Workshop 1:N Appointment)

userId.description: Reference to the User. (Relationship: User 1:N Appointment)

appointmentDateTime.description: Date and time of the appointment.

description.description: Customer provided notes regarding the appointment.

status.description: Status of the appointment (e.g., scheduled, completed, cancelled).

User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the user entity.

Service.title: Service
Service.description: Represents a service offered by a specific workshop.

id.description: Unique identifier for the service entity.

name.description: Name of the service (e.g., Oil Change, Tire Rotation).

Review.title: Review
Review.description: Represents a customer review for a workshop.

id.description: Unique identifier for the review entity.

workshopId.description: Reference to the Workshop. (Relationship: Workshop 1:N Review)

userId.description: Reference to the User who wrote the review.

comment.description: The text content of the review.

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the review was created.

OilChange.title: OilChange
OilChange.description: Represents an oil change record for a user's vehicle.

id.description: Unique identifier for the oil change record.

userId.description: Reference to the User. (Relationship: User 1:N OilChange)

vehicleId.description: Reference to the Vehicle. (Relationship: Vehicle 1:N OilChange)

vehicleName.description: Denormalized name of the vehicle for easy display.

mileage.description: Vehicle mileage at the time of the change.

nextChangeMileage.description: Recommended mileage for the next oil change.

id.description: Unique identifier for the vehicle entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who owns the vehicle.

type.description: Type of vehicle (e.g., Sedan, SUV, Truck).

brand.description: The brand of the vehicle (e.g., Toyota, Ford).

model.description: The model of the vehicle (e.g., Camry, F-150).

year.description: The manufacturing year of the vehicle.

currentMileage.description: The current mileage of the vehicle.

imageUrls.description: A list of URLs for the vehicle's images.

isForSale.description: Indicates if the vehicle is for sale in the marketplace.

country.description: Country where the vehicle is located.

FavoriteWorkshop.title: FavoriteWorkshop
FavoriteWorkshop.description: Represents a user's favorite workshop, with denormalized data for easy display.

workshopId.description: The ID of the workshop that is favorited.

imageUrl.description: Denormalized image URL of the workshop.

averageRating.description: Denormalized average rating of the workshop.

addedAt.description: Timestamp of when the workshop was added to favorites.

definition.description: Stores details of mechanic workshops. Includes denormalized 'obdScannerService' boolean to enable secure filtering for workshops offering OBDII services.

params.name: workshopId
params.description: The unique identifier for the workshop.

definition.description: Stores customer reviews for a specific workshop.

params.name: workshopId
params.description: The unique identifier for the workshop.

params.name: reviewId
params.description: The unique identifier for the review.

definition.description: Stores the services offered by a specific workshop.

params.name: workshopId
params.description: The unique identifier for the workshop.

params.name: serviceId
params.description: The unique identifier for the service.

definition.description: Stores user profile information. Each user has a unique ID.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

definition.description: Stores appointment data for the related User.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: appointmentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the appointment.

definition.description: Stores oil change records for a user's vehicle.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: oilChangeId
params.description: The unique identifier for the oil change record.

definition.description: Stores vehicle records for a specific user.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: vehicleId
params.description: The unique identifier for the vehicle record.

definition.description: Stores a user's favorite workshops. The document ID is the workshopId for easy lookup.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: workshopId
params.description: The unique identifier for the favorited workshop.

definition.description: Stores copies of vehicles listed for sale. This allows public read access for the marketplace while keeping the original user vehicle data private.

params.name: vehicleId
params.description: The unique identifier for the vehicle, same as in the user's subcollection.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure is designed to support the MechConnect application, focusing on mechanic workshop registration, appointment booking, and OBDII scanner service highlighting. The structure prioritizes authorization independence and supports QAPs (Rules are not Filters) to ensure secure `list` operations. Path-based ownership is used where applicable to simplify security rules. The structure is organized into collections for `Workshops` and `Users`, with subcollections to manage relationships effectively.

**Authorization Independence:**

*   **Appointments:** The `appointments` subcollection under each `user` document includes the `userId` of the user who created the appointment. This denormalization is critical for authorization independence because security rules can authorize access to appointments based on the `userId` in the appointment document and/or ownership of the parent `User` document. Without the `userId` on the `Appointment`, rules would need to perform a `get()` to verify the requesting user owns the parent `User` document, violating Authorization Independence.
*   **Reviews**: Reviews are stored in a subcollection under each workshop (`/workshops/{workshopId}/reviews`). This allows for easy retrieval of all reviews for a specific workshop and enables security rules that grant write access only to authenticated users.
* **Services**: Services are stored in a subcollection under each workshop (`/workshops/{workshopId}/services`). This allows each workshop to manage its own list of services and prices, and makes it easy to retrieve all services for a specific workshop.
* **Favorites**: Favorite workshops are stored in a subcollection under each user (`/users/{userId}/favorites`). This ensures that only the owner can read or write their own favorites list, enforcing strict data privacy.

**QAPs Support:**

*   **Workshops Collection:** All workshops are stored in a single `workshops` collection, but segregation is achieved via the `obdScannerService` field. List operations can be secured by filtering based on workshop attributes in the rules (e.g., `obdScannerService == true`) to highlight workshops offering OBDII scanner services, without compromising security.
*   **User-Owned Data:** Storing appointments, oil changes, vehicles, and favorites within a user's document ensures that only the user has access to their own data.

**Marketplace:**
* **Marketplace Collection:** A top-level `marketplace` collection stores denormalized copies of vehicles listed for sale. This is crucial for security and performance. It allows unauthenticated users to browse all vehicles for sale (`allow list`) without being able to access the private `/users` collection. Security rules ensure that only the vehicle's owner can create, update, or delete their listing in the marketplace, maintaining data integrity.
